channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump tariffs spark 'exciting time' for Ohio steel plant as CEO eyes adding jobs, boosting productivity
item.description: The CEO of JSW USA , Robert Simon, spoke with Fox News Digital in an exclusive interview celebrating President Trump's reciprocal tariffs as leveling the playing field.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: President Donald Trump has threatened tariffs on semiconductors, arguing that production must be returned to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some in Hurricane Helene-ravaged North Carolina embrace Trump's push to abolish FEMA
item.description: President Donald Trump's suggestion about “getting rid of” the nation's disaster relief agency doesn't sound like a bad idea for some residents of western North Carolina who are still navigating the long recovery process from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rubio, Netanyahu affirm 'common strategy' for Gaza, set sights on Iran in joint statement
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says he shares a "common strategy" for Gaza with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congressman urges Trump admin to help ailing Area 51 veterans
item.description: “What I saw was disturbing. I am angered,” Rep. Mark Takano, D-Calif., says about the plight of Area 51 vets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: German chancellor slams Vance's call for Europe to be inclusive of far-right parties
item.description: Olaf Scholz rebuked U.S. Vice President JD Vance for suggesting that far-right parties should not be shut out of government. Germany's other political parties have refused to work with the far-right AfD.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim and Rob Schmitz, MPR News

item.title: DOGE scores big court win, allowed access data on 3 federal agencies
item.description: A federal judge in Washington on Friday handed Elon Musk's government efficiency team a win by declining a request to temporarily block it from accessing sensitive data from at least three federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hungary's transformation into an 'electoral autocracy' has parallels to Trump's second term
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hours before President Donald Trump was sworn in to begin his second term, promising a “golden age” for America, the leader of a Central European country was describing the years ahead in strikingly similar terms.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New poll reveals growing number of Dems want party to move in new direction
item.description: After the Democratic Party's setbacks to Trump and Republicans in the 2024 elections, a new national poll indicates Democrats increasingly want their party to move toward the center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EEOC seeks to drop transgender discrimination cases, citing Trump's executive order
item.description: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission has moved to dismiss six of its own cases on behalf of workers alleging gender identity discrimination, arguing that the cases now conflict with President Donald Trump’s recent executive order.
item.dc:creator: Claire Savage And Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scrivner’s legal woes shattered family political network
item.description: The saga surrounding Zack Scrivner isn't just a story about his alleged crimes. He was one of the most well-connected politicians in Kern County history.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department asks court to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department asked a court Friday to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, with a top official from Washington intervening after federal prosecutors in Manhattan rebuffed his demands to drop the case and some quit in protest.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alleged DOGE employees show up at San Francisco City Hall
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- An official with the San Francisco Sheriff’s Office confirmed to NewsNation affiliate KRON4 that it is investigating a report of three individuals claiming to be Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) employees who attempted to enter offices in San Francisco City Hall on
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Trump creates council for 'energy dominance,' boosts natural gas exports and offshore drilling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday signed an executive order formally creating a National Energy Dominance Council and directed it to move quickly to drive up already record-setting domestic oil and gas production.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Annexing Canada? At Niagara Falls, Trump's idea gets eye rolls but not support
item.description: NIAGARA FALLS, N.Y. (AP) — At Niagara Falls, the iconic honeymoon and tourist destination on the U.S.-Canadian border, visitors from both countries took in President Donald Trump’s unsolicited suggestion that the U.S. annex its neighbor to the north with an air of detached amusement.
item.dc:creator: MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An open Minnesota Senate seat is enticing to plenty but unclear how many will take plunge
item.description: Minnesota has its first open-seat Senate race since 2006. Many candidates are giving it a look and both parties say they’re well-positioned to win.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Can suspending a cage-free egg law solve the soaring price problem? Nevada takes a crack at it
item.description: Record high egg prices prompted Nevada lawmakers to unanimously back suspending their cage-free egg law, but the six other states with similar laws in effect have shown no sign of dropping them even if it might help lower grocery bills.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Sejal Govindarao And Ty Oneil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
